title: Doe WGSL backend equivalence crosswalk
description: Crosswalk artifact asserting a single WGSL compute source has semantically equivalent implementations across multiple Doe backends (CSL via memcpy runtime, CSL via SdkLayout streaming executor, SPIR-V via naga/spirv-val, etc.). Each backend entry points to the emitter, the compiled artifact, and whatever runtime-execution evidence exists. This artifact is the load-bearing reader-facing claim that 'the same JS/WGSL program runs on Cerebras and Vulkan via one shared Doe IR'. Regenerated by bench/tools/generate_wgsl_backend_equivalence.py by reading the component evidence artifacts (dual-compile-evidence/**/evidence.json, kernel-chain-evidence/**/chain-parity.json, streaming-executor/**/*-trace.json) and sha256-binding them.

kernelId.description: Stable identifier for the WGSL kernel (e.g. 'elementwise-double').

backend.description: Free-form identifier — e.g. csl-memcpy, csl-sdklayout, spirv, hlsl, msl.

staticValidation.description: Static (non-execution) validator output — e.g. spirv-val, cslc compile-only.

sharedSemantics.description: One-line statement of the operation, e.g. 'output[i] = input[i] * 2.0'.

executionProvenBitExact.description: Backends whose execution evidence is bit-exact (maxAbsErr=0) against the WGSL semantics.

executionProvenBitClose.description: Backends whose execution evidence is bit-close (0 < maxAbsErr < 1e-5) — expected for kernels with non-associative reductions where PE-distributed ordering differs from numpy reference.

executionNotYetWired.description: Backends that compile but don't yet have end-to-end execution evidence.
